title: Unit B-2 Self-Check (3 questions, for confirming understanding -- no pass/fail record is saved)

questions.q: What is the most appropriate benefit of instructing generative AI, "If you need more information, please ask me first"?
choices: It prevents the conversation from proceeding with the AI having silently filled in missing assumptions with its own guesses
It makes the AI feel faster to work with, since more back-and-forth exchanges take place
The AI will remember your preferences from then on and apply them automatically next time
Simply giving that one instruction automatically guarantees that the output will be medically accurate
questions.explanation: When information is missing, the AI fills the gaps with guesses to produce its output. Having it ask questions first lets you draw out output based on the actual situation (audience, purpose, constraints) instead of guesswork. This is unrelated to guaranteeing accuracy or to the AI remembering things.

questions.q: When having generative AI critique your own deliverable, which approach is most appropriate for drawing out candid feedback?
choices: Asking the AI to mention only the positive points of the piece, and to leave out anything that sounds critical
Specifying the role and format: "as a strict reviewer, point out three important things to improve, in order, with reasons"
Showing it the same piece again and again, unchanged, until you get an evaluation you like
Deciding that critique is something only a human expert should provide, not an AI, and skipping the step of asking the AI for feedback
questions.explanation: Generative AI tends to respond favorably, so specifying a reviewer role, the number of points, and a requirement to state reasons makes concrete, critical feedback easier to draw out instead of flattery. Also specifying the aspects you want evaluated (structure, evidence, length, and so on) is even more effective.

questions.q: What is the most appropriate way to respond after receiving critique from the AI?
choices: Treat the AI's points as equal in weight to a senior expert's opinion, and apply each point as-is without question or double-checking
Treat the AI's points as merely a machine's opinion, and dismiss them without even reading them
Judge the validity of each point yourself and adopt the ones you find convincing (the final decision is made by a human)
Ask the same AI whether its own point was correct, and treat that reply as the final word
questions.explanation: Even AI critique can include points that are off-base or mistaken. Neither taking the critique at face value nor ignoring it outright is correct -- the right approach is to judge the validity of each point yourself and pick and choose accordingly (this connects to the "human verification" you'll learn about in Area C).
